content: Priya works on Stratus. Frontend: React + TypeScript strict mode, Tailwind utilities, react-query for client state, fetch + a thin wrapper (no axios). Backend: FastAPI + SQLModel + Pydantic v2 + asyncpg.
summary: Stratus stack: React/TS-strict/Tailwind/react-query frontend; FastAPI/SQLModel/Pydantic v2/asyncpg backend.

content: TypeScript strict mode is enforced; never suggest `any`. Prefer explicit types and Pydantic models for backend examples. Style preference: composition over inheritance — hooks over HOCs in React, protocols + injection over ABCs in Python.
summary: TS strict, no `any`, explicit types. Composition > inheritance: hooks > HOCs, protocols > ABCs.

content: Code-review checklist for Priya: (1) tests pass, (2) no console.log, (3) no commented-out code. Lead any review with these three checks before commenting on style. PR #458 is the canonical example — all three items flagged plus a missing test.
summary: Review checklist: tests pass / no console.log / no commented-out code. Lead with these three before style.

content: Test policy: never mock the database. DB-touching tests run against a real Postgres in CI (pytest fixtures or pytest-postgresql). Established after a 2026-03-18 incident where mocked-DB tests passed but a `customer_id → account_id` migration shipped broken.
summary: Integration tests > mocks. Real Postgres in CI. (Incident 2026-03-18: mocks hid migration break.)

content: Async style at Stratus: top-level async functions, await chains, no .then() chains, no asyncio.run() inside library code. Tests use pytest-asyncio with async test functions, not unittest-style + asyncio.run.
summary: Top-level async + pytest-asyncio. No .then(), no asyncio.run() in library code.

content: Styling: Tailwind utilities only. Don't suggest CSS-in-JS, styled-components, or emotion — Stratus tried CSS-in-JS in 2025 and rolled back due to bundle size.
summary: Tailwind only. No CSS-in-JS / styled-components / emotion (rolled back in 2025).

content: Architecture decision (ADR-007, 2026-03-13): the new pricing service uses Postgres + SQLModel. Reasons: shared transactions with invoicing, existing SRE familiarity, no document-shaped writes. MongoDB was explicitly rejected.
summary: ADR-007: Pricing service on Postgres + SQLModel (Mongo rejected).

content: Fix shape from PR #389 (session-pool race): if you see intermittent 500s under concurrent load, look at session-pool acquisition first. Fix is a lock around acquire(), pool_pre_ping=True, and a 100-concurrent invariant test. The same shape may recur in other services.
summary: Intermittent 500s under load → session-pool race. Fix: lock + pool_pre_ping=True + 100-concurrent invariant test (PR #389).

content: Feature-flag any Dashboards V2 work behind FLAG_DASHBOARD_V2 — both client (LaunchDarkly React SDK) and server. New routes, components, and endpoints all gated.
summary: Dashboards V2 work gates behind FLAG_DASHBOARD_V2 (LaunchDarkly React SDK).

content: Diff-size convention: keep PRs concise. If a refactor exceeds ~150 lines, propose splitting into a stack of smaller PRs first. Priya prefers reviewing three small PRs over one large one.
summary: PRs ≤150 lines; propose a stack split before exceeding that.

content: Commit messages use conventional-commits prefixes only: feat:, fix:, chore:, refactor:, test:, docs:.

Auth-middleware refactor in flight as of late April. Goal: collapse JWT verification + session-pool acquisition (currently across 4 files) into one FastAPI dependency. Blocker: one of the current files runs alembic migrations on module import (circular dep). The plan is to lift migrations into the FastAPI lifespan handler first, then extract the dependency.
summary: In-flight: auth-middleware → single FastAPI Depends. Blocker: migrations on import; lift to lifespan first.

content: Shared TS types live in the `@stratus/types` package (monorepo). Frontend and the TypeScript SDK both consume it; new domain types go there, not in app packages.
summary: Shared types in `@stratus/types` (consumed by FE + TS SDK).

content: Build / deploy: pnpm + turbo monorepo. Frontend deploys to Vercel; backend to Fly.io in regions iad + lhr. CI is GitHub Actions. Rate-limit middleware decision: sliding-window in Redis, per-API-key (closed decision; do not re-litigate).
summary: Build: pnpm + turbo. Deploy: Vercel FE, Fly.io iad+lhr BE. CI: GH Actions. Rate-limit: Redis sliding-window per API key (closed).
